units.about: Changes only how values are shown. All calculations run internally in mm.
units.precision: Rounds display values to common fractions of an inch. Does not affect calculations.

frets.count: Number of frets to calculate, draw, and export.

scaleLength.fundamental: Nut-to-bridge scale length for the reference string.
scaleLength.first: Scale length on the first-string side (usually treble).
scaleLength.last: Scale length on the last-string side (usually bass).
scaleLength.perpendicular: Where the frets are perpendicular to the centerline: 0 = nut, 1 = bridge.

strings.count: Total strings for spacing and per-string calculations.
strings.nutWidth: Outer-string spread at the nut (center-to-center of string 1 to string N).
strings.bridgeWidth: Outer-string spread at the bridge (center-to-center of string 1 to string N).
strings.spacing: Equal uses uniform gaps. Proportional adjusts gaps based on string gauge.
strings.gauges: String gauges in inches, used only for proportional spacing.

overhang.about: Distance from the outer string center to the fretboard edge.
overhang.nutExtension: How far the fretboard wood extends past the nut toward the headstock. 0 = flush with nut.
overhang.lastFretExtension: How far the fretboard extends past the last fret toward the body. 10mm is typical.

calculation.tonesPerOctave: 12 is standard. Use other values for equal temperament (19, 24, 31...).
calculation.scalaInput: Paste a .scl file. The interval count must match the declared number.
calculation.tuning: For Scala: which scale step each string uses as its open string (0 = unison).

export.extendFrets: Extends fret lines to the fretboard outline for cleaner CNC/print exports.

errors.invalidScala: Invalid Scala file. Check that the number of intervals matches the declared count.

url.invalid: Invalid share link. Loaded defaults instead.

donations.title: Did you find this tool useful?
